cards.title: Sundar Pichai Faces Protests at Stanford Graduation
cards.body: Google's CEO was booed and faced a walkout at Stanford's graduation ceremony over the company's defense contracts and AI use. Protesters are raising concerns about technology's involvement in military and ICE applications.

cards.title: Trump Admin Bans Anthropic's Advanced Cybersecurity Models
cards.body: The U.S. government forced Anthropic to pull its latest models, signaling that AI companies aren't immune from government interference. The decision appears reactionary, but reflects growing regulatory scrutiny of the AI industry.

cards.title: Meta Launches AI Mode on Facebook Platform
cards.body: Meta announced new AI features on Facebook that pull from public information across its platforms. The rollout represents the company's latest effort to compete in the AI race and boost user engagement.

cards.title: SpaceX Goes Public with Historic IPO
cards.body: SpaceX completed its highly anticipated IPO, marking a major milestone for the aerospace company. The listing includes details on pre-IPO deals and the company's growth trajectory.

cards.title: Cybersecurity Experts Protest Anthropic Model Ban
cards.body: Dozens of cybersecurity veterans urged the White House to lift export controls on Anthropic's Fable and Mythos models. They argue the restrictions will hinder defenders' ability to secure software and products.

cards.title: Salesforce Acquires AI Platform Fin for $3.6B
cards.body: Salesforce announced the acquisition of customer service AI platform Fin to strengthen its Agentforce enterprise offering. The deal will help businesses build custom AI agents to automate tasks.

cards.title: Sarvam AI Becomes India's Newest Unicorn
cards.body: Indian IT services company HCLTech invested $150 million in Bengaluru-based AI startup Sarvam as part of a $234 million funding round. The investment underscores India's growing AI ambitions.

cards.title: NewCore Raises $66M to Manage AI Agents
cards.body: NewCore secured $66 million funding to tackle enterprise security challenges posed by AI agents as employees. The startup argues managing AI identities will be the next critical frontier.

cards.title: Earth Observation Satellite Achieves Autonomous Discovery
cards.body: In April, an Earth observation satellite successfully found its target entirely on its own for the first time. The achievement represents a major milestone in autonomous space technology.

cards.title: AI Layoff Wave Creates Wealth Inequality Crisis
cards.body: Tens of thousands of AI workers face job cuts while a small cohort of industry insiders accumulates unprecedented wealth. The disparity is creating growing tensions within the sector.

cards.title: AI Startups Rush to Go Public Post-SpaceX IPO
cards.body: Multiple AI companies are attempting to capitalize on investor momentum following SpaceX's successful public debut. Startups hope to ride the wave of renewed market enthusiasm.

cards.title: Anthropic Suspension Prompts India AI Debate
cards.body: As Anthropic suspends access to new models, Indian tech leaders are questioning the country's AI independence. The episode serves as a potential wake-up call for India's strategic AI position.

cards.title: Meta Unwinds $2B Manus Deal After Beijing Pressure
cards.body: Meta is moving to divest its Manus acquisition following Beijing's national security divestiture order. The step represents concrete compliance with the directive issued two months prior.

cards.title: KPMG Pulls AI Report Over Model Hallucinations
cards.body: KPMG withdrew a report due to apparent hallucinations generated by AI systems used in its analysis. The incident highlights ongoing reliability concerns with AI-generated content.

cards.title: Amazon CEO May Have Triggered Anthropic Crackdown
cards.body: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy reportedly raised security concerns about Anthropic's models before the government crackdown. This may have been instrumental in prompting the suspension of two advanced models.

cards.title: State Attorneys General Investigate OpenAI
cards.body: Multiple state attorneys general have launched investigations into OpenAI covering ad policies and health data handling. The scope of the inquiry remains broad and details are still emerging.

cards.title: Yang Identifies Startup Gold Rush in Cost Reduction
cards.body: Andrew Yang believes the next major startup opportunity lies in lowering American living costs. He's identified housing, food, and wireless as prime targets for disruption.

cards.title: Anthropic's Safety Warnings Backfire on Company
cards.body: The government pulled Anthropic's most powerful models after the company disclosed a narrow potential jailbreak. Anthropic disputes the decision, arguing the finding doesn't justify recalling commercially deployed models.

cards.title: Meta's AI Unit Faces Morale Crisis, Potential Revolt
cards.body: Meta's 6,500-person AI unit is reportedly experiencing severe morale problems described as a 'soul-crushing gulag'. Engineers within the division are reportedly on the verge of open revolt.

cards.title: Google Sues Chinese Cybercrime Group Using AI Scams
cards.body: Google filed suit against 'Outsider Enterprise,' a Chinese cybercrime operation that used AI to defraud hundreds of thousands. The group sent 2.5 million scam text messages over just two weeks.

cards.title: Meta's AI Mode Pulls Data Across Its Platforms
cards.body: Meta is rolling out new AI features on Facebook to compete in the AI race and boost user engagement. The features leverage public information across Meta's platform ecosystem.

cards.title: Salesforce Acquires AI Customer Service Platform Fin
cards.body: Salesforce is acquiring Fin for $3.6B to enhance its Agentforce platform. The deal aims to improve enterprise AI agents that automate customer service tasks.

cards.title: Sarvam Becomes India's Newest AI Unicorn
cards.body: Indian startup Sarvam reached unicorn status with a $234M funding round led by HCLTech. HCLTech invested $150M in the Bengaluru-based AI company.

cards.title: NewCore Raises $66M for AI Agent Identity Management
cards.body: NewCore secured $66M to address enterprise security challenges posed by AI agents. The startup argues managing AI agent identities is the next frontier in corporate security.

cards.title: Earth Observation Satellite Finds Targets Autonomously
cards.body: A satellite successfully located its target independently for the first time in April. The achievement marks a significant milestone in autonomous AI-powered Earth observation.

cards.title: AI Layoff Wave Creates Massive Wealth Inequality
cards.body: Thousands of AI workers face layoffs while a small cohort of insiders becomes extraordinarily wealthy. The disparity highlights growing tensions within the AI industry.

cards.title: Anthropic Suspends Access to Latest AI Models
cards.body: Anthropic suspended access to its Fable and Mythos models following a Trump administration directive. The company disputes the government's decision, citing only a narrow potential jailbreak.

cards.title: KPMG Pulls AI Report Due to Hallucinations
cards.body: KPMG retracted a report after discovering apparent AI hallucinations compromised its accuracy. The incident underscores ongoing reliability challenges with AI-generated content.

cards.title: OpenAI Faces Investigation from State Attorneys
cards.body: Multiple state attorneys general are investigating OpenAI regarding ad policies and health data handling. The scope and nature of the investigation remain unclear.

cards.title: Chinese Cybercrime Ring Used AI for Mass Scams
cards.body: Google sued a Chinese cybercrime group called 'Outsider Enterprise' for using AI to defraud hundreds of thousands. The operation sent 2.5M text messages over two weeks.

cards.title: Brain Implant Enables ALS Patient to Communicate
cards.body: Casey Harrell has used a brain-computer interface for nearly three years to speak with AI assistance. The paralyzed ALS patient represents a breakthrough in neural implant technology.

cards.title: Google DeepMind Studies Risks of AI Agent Interactions
cards.body: Google DeepMind is researching potential dangers when millions of AI agents interact online. AGI safety researcher Rohin Shah leads efforts addressing mass-market AI agent deployment risks.

cards.title: Anthropic Shuts Down Fable, Mythos Models
cards.body: Following Trump administration directive, Anthropic permanently shut down its Fable and Mythos AI models. The move reflects government interference in the commercial AI sector.
